text: her shoulder, generating additional warmth. “That’s no small desire, Lara Brooks .” “We have the world ahead of us, don’t we?” She tilted her head back and smirked up at him, though he couldn’t see it through her scarf . “All of it,” he agreed, “but I reserve the right not to take you to certain parts .” “What parts ?” “The Dust is only one part of this world. A big one, to be certain, and there’s not much worse…but there’s not very much better, either. There are places that would kill you if you went too close .” Lara nuzzled her cheek against him, closing her eyes and calling up the pictures of the ocean. “As long as I’m with you, I don’t care which parts we skip .” Her words drifted away on the wind, and they walked in comfortable silence for a long while. Lara’s eyelids were heavy, and her legs grew more so with each step. His hold on her tightened as she gradually sagged against him . “How did you learn to dance the way you do ?” His question jarred her out of near-sleep, and her tired mind struggled to understand what he was asking . “When I was little, I saw a woman dancing. I spied on her…like you did to me. I didn’t know what to make of it, only that it looked beautiful. So, I started doing it, too. And I felt so…free. I loved it .” “I’ve never seen anything like it. Women dance at places like Kitty’s, but that doesn’t compare to what you do .” “Places like Kitty’s, huh?” A sudden weight formed in her gut, and a fire sparked in her chest. How many women had he watched ? How many of them had he fucked ? “Most settlements have similar establishments. Never held much interest for me. They usually look so…lifeless on stage .” “Probably not so lifeless in bed,” she muttered. She didn’t like this feeling, this jealousy , but it wouldn’t go away. Clenching her teeth, she pulled away. Lara knew she had no right to be upset about his past, but she couldn’t stand the thought of him making love to another person — or bot — as he did with her . Ronin didn’t let her get far before he drew her back against his side. It was difficult to protest, despite her fiery emotions, given the heat he put out . “The last female I coupled with, before you, was another synth. That was four thousand, one hundred and twelve days before I met you. When we finished, we each went our way without a backward glance .” Lara wasn’t surprised that he knew it down to the day, but had it really been that long ? “What about human women?” She didn’t want to know, not really, but the question came out anyway . “There’ve been two, over the years. Both the same situation as with the synth. There was never a connection with any of them .” As easy as it would be to get angry at him, to accuse him of lying about the nature of his past couplings , she couldn’t. He was being honest, and how could she fault him for things that probably happened before she was even born? His mind captured everything , every minute detail of every experience . And, despite the women he had sex with in the past, it was Lara who he’d chosen to be with. He cared for her, and she loved him . “I’m sure I was active before the Black Out, too, though I do not know for how long or what purpose I might have served then . “ “It doesn’t matter, right? The past is in the past.” Saying it out loud helped her remaining tension ease. If she stopped to think about it really hard, she was sure she’d still be jealous of those other women, but that couldn’t be helped. Ronin was hers . “Was the woman you watched different, too?” he asked . “ Different how ?” “You move with emotion. Lose yourself in it. Was she the same ?” “Yeah.” The memories were fuzzy, like they were from a different life. “At Kitty’s, we danced because we had to. That was the only way we could earn credits to buy food. Nobody was there to watch us express our feelings. They just wanted us to shake our tits around .” “I do enjoy your breasts,” Ronin said thoughtfully. Lara smacked his chest. “But it wasn’t your body that first caught my attention. It was the way you moved it. That dance spoke everything in your heart before you said your first fuck off to me .” She laughed, shaking her head. “That’s not the first thing I said to you .” “No. First, you asked who I was. Then it was get out , followed by I know what you are, you’re a — ” “Okay, okay, I get it. I don’t know if that memory of yours is a good thing or not.” She peeked up at him briefly before burying her face in his warmth . “I remember everything that’s happened since I woke. All the pain, the struggles, the terrible scenes. But all the good, too. There’s not nearly as much of that…but it seems to go a lot further .” “Will you remember me, when I’m gone ?” His stride faltered, body going rigid before he stopped. She looked up again to find his eyes on her . “Even through another Black Out, I could never forget you, Lara Brooks .” Her eyes stung, and she couldn’t blame it on the wind this time. She hid her face again, refusing to let him see her cry. Did he love her ? They resumed their walk. He’d successfully distracted her; her mind was awake and racing, examining things he’d said to her, things he’d done for her
